fieldRegistry.label: Primary Reason For Home Health / Medical Necessity

fieldGroups.label: Medical Necessity And Homebound
fieldGroups.description: Narratives and evidence for home health need and homebound status.

fieldGroups.label: Diagnosis And Coding
fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

abilities to take care of her ADLs on her own.
propel it due to arthritis in her shoulders and also due to her excessive weight.

sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is an 80-year-old female with severe functional and medical impairments that prevent safe, independent leaving of the home without assistance.

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient is considered homebound because she is an 80-year-old female with severe functional and medical impairments that prevent safe, independent leaving of the home without assistance.

sourceHighlights.title: Prior level of function
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient previously had a live-in caregiver, but is now living alone and requires assistance with activities of daily living.

sourceHighlights.title: Wound history
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has chronic venous stasis dermatitis and is being monitored for potential development of open wounds.

sourceHighlights.title: Diet and fluid instructions
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has been advised to limit caloric intake and increase activity as tolerated due to morbid obesity.

sourceHighlights.title: PMH, immunizations, and DM status
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has a history of morbid obesity, chronic kidney disease, and has received COVID-19 vaccinations.

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references
sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has been diagnosed with bilateral primary osteoarthritis of the knees, chronic venous stasis dermatitis, impaired mobility, morbid obesity, and chronic kidney disease.
supportingSections: Diagnoses and possible coding support references

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is an 80-year-old female with severe functional and medical impairments that prevent safe, independent leaving of the home without assistance. She uses a walker for mobility but requires a power chair for assistance with activities of daily living due to her limited ambulation abilities.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is considered homebound because she is an 80-year-old female with severe functional and medical impairments that prevent safe, independent leaving of the home without assistance. She requires physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: This 80-year-old female is being seen at home for follow-up on chronic health conditions. She has a history of morbid obesity, chronic kidney disease, and chronic venous stasis dermatitis. She is requesting a power chair for assistance with activities of daily living due to her limited ambulation abilities. She is also requesting physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status. She also requires a power chair for assistance with activities of daily living due to her limited ambulation abilities.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: The patient will continue to be monitored for chronic health conditions, including chronic kidney disease and chronic venous stasis dermatitis. She will also continue to receive physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: The patient's care plan includes monitoring for chronic health conditions, including chronic kidney disease and chronic venous stasis dermatitis. She will also receive physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status. The goal is to improve her functional status and quality of life.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: The patient's goal is to improve her functional status and quality of life. She is requesting a power chair for assistance with activities of daily living due to her limited ambulation abilities. She is also requesting physical therapy for assistance with ambulation due to decreased mobility and decline in functional status.
